houston-tx.major_employer: ExxonMobil, Shell, Chevron, Texas Medical Center
houston-tx.local_fact: Houston is home to the Texas Medical Center — the world's largest medical complex — whose 106,000 employees make it one of the city's top daily fuel consumers
houston-tx.commute_note: Average Houston commute is 30 minutes — one of the longest in Texas — driving fuel demand across all major corridors
houston-tx.price_factor: Proximity to Gulf Coast refineries and the Houston Ship Channel keeps fuel distribution costs among the lowest in Texas

san-antonio-tx.refinery_name: CITGO Corpus Christi pipeline network, Valero HQ San Antonio
san-antonio-tx.military_base_name: Joint Base San Antonio (Lackland, Randolph, Fort Sam Houston) — ~36,000 active-duty
san-antonio-tx.major_employer: USAA, Valero Energy HQ, H-E-B HQ, Joint Base San Antonio
san-antonio-tx.local_fact: San Antonio is home to Valero Energy's corporate headquarters and to H-E-B's headquarters — two Texas fuel-economy giants based in the same city
san-antonio-tx.commute_note: I-35 through San Antonio runs from Laredo to the DFW area and carries heavy NAFTA freight traffic that meets local commuters on the same roads
san-antonio-tx.price_factor: Local Valero and H-E-B competition plus pipeline access from Gulf Coast refineries keeps SA prices close to the state average

dallas-tx.major_employer: AT&T HQ, Texas Instruments, Southwest Airlines, DFW Airport
dallas-tx.local_fact: Dallas hosts AT&T's corporate HQ, and DFW International Airport just west of the city is the 4th busiest in the world — both massive fuel consumers
dallas-tx.commute_note: Dallas commuters average 28 minutes with heavy congestion on US-75 Central Expressway and LBJ Freeway
dallas-tx.price_factor: Dallas is 250+ miles from the nearest refinery, so pipeline transport adds to pump prices compared to Gulf Coast cities

austin-tx.university_name: University of Texas at Austin (53,000 students)
austin-tx.major_employer: Dell Technologies, Tesla Gigafactory, Apple, Samsung, State of Texas, UT Austin
austin-tx.local_fact: Austin hosts Tesla's Gigafactory Texas, Samsung's $17B semiconductor fab in nearby Taylor (30 miles east), and Apple's second-largest US campus — tech growth has driven population gains straining I-35 daily
austin-tx.commute_note: I-35 through Austin is one of the most congested segments in Texas, with traffic often crawling from Round Rock through downtown to Buda
austin-tx.price_factor: Austin prices trend 2-5¢ above the state average due to distance from Gulf refineries and high per-capita demand from a wealthy commuter base

fort-worth-tx.military_base_name: Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base Fort Worth
fort-worth-tx.major_employer: Lockheed Martin (F-35 production), American Airlines, BNSF Railway HQ
fort-worth-tx.local_fact: Lockheed Martin's Fort Worth plant builds every F-35 fighter in the US inventory, employing 18,000 workers who commute primarily via I-35W and I-820
fort-worth-tx.commute_note: BNSF Railway HQ and the Alliance intermodal hub in north Fort Worth generate heavy truck traffic on I-35W 24/7
fort-worth-tx.price_factor: Fort Worth prices track Dallas closely due to shared DFW pipeline distribution; Alliance area typically has slightly cheaper gas than downtown

el-paso-tx.military_base_name: Fort Bliss (1.12 million acres, one of largest US Army posts)
el-paso-tx.university_name: University of Texas at El Paso (25,000 students)
el-paso-tx.major_employer: Fort Bliss, William Beaumont Army Medical Center, UTEP, El Paso Electric
el-paso-tx.local_fact: El Paso sits 800 miles from Houston — it's the westernmost major Texas city and home to Fort Bliss, one of the largest US Army installations at 1.12 million acres
el-paso-tx.commute_note: I-10 through El Paso is the main artery from LA to Jacksonville; Ciudad Juárez across the border adds cross-border commuter traffic at the Bridge of the Americas
el-paso-tx.price_factor: Local Marathon refinery and proximity to New Mexico fuel markets keep El Paso prices below the Texas state average most weeks

arlington-tx.university_name: University of Texas at Arlington (42,000 students)
arlington-tx.major_employer: General Motors Assembly, AT&T Stadium, Globe Life Field, Six Flags, UTA
arlington-tx.local_fact: Arlington is the entertainment capital of DFW — AT&T Stadium (Cowboys), Globe Life Field (Rangers), and Six Flags Over Texas each draw millions of visitors per year
arlington-tx.commute_note: Arlington sits midway between Dallas and Fort Worth and has no passenger rail, so nearly all its 390,000 residents drive — among the highest per-capita vehicle rates in the metroplex
arlington-tx.price_factor: Prices between Dallas and Fort Worth average — Arlington typically sits within 2¢ of the metro mean with competition from interstate-adjacent stations

corpus-christi-tx.refinery_name: Flint Hills Resources Corpus Christi — approximately 350,000 bbl/day combined
corpus-christi-tx.major_employer: Flint Hills Resources, Valero refinery, CITGO, Port of Corpus Christi, NAS Corpus Christi
corpus-christi-tx.local_fact: The Port of Corpus Christi is the largest US port for crude oil exports by volume, handling over 2 million barrels of petroleum products daily
corpus-christi-tx.commute_note: Heavy refinery truck traffic on I-37 and US-181 creates consistent daily freight volume through Corpus Christi
corpus-christi-tx.price_factor: Three local refineries (Flint Hills, Valero, CITGO) and direct pipeline access make Corpus Christi gas prices among the cheapest in Texas

plano-tx.major_employer: Toyota North America HQ, JP Morgan Chase, Liberty Mutual, Frito-Lay HQ, Capital One
plano-tx.local_fact: Plano hosts Toyota Motor North America's headquarters — relocated from California in 2017 — along with Liberty Mutual, JP Morgan Chase, and Capital One campuses in the Legacy West development
plano-tx.commute_note: Plano commuters drive US-75 and the Dallas North Tollway into Legacy West and downtown Dallas, with median income among the highest in DFW
plano-tx.price_factor: High-income suburb: prices trend 3-5¢ above the metro average at upscale brand stations, with Costco Plano driving discount competition

lubbock-tx.major_employer: Texas Tech University, Covenant Health System, United Supermarkets HQ
lubbock-tx.local_fact: Lubbock is the agricultural hub of the South Plains — Texas Tech's 40,000 students and the surrounding cotton/cattle economy anchor the regional economy
lubbock-tx.commute_note: Short commutes (average 17 minutes) but high ag-truck and pickup ownership keep per-capita fuel demand high
lubbock-tx.price_factor: 120+ miles from the nearest refinery terminal adds trucking cost; prices typically run a few cents above the state average

irving-tx.major_employer: Kimberly-Clark HQ, McKesson HQ, Fluor Corporation, Celanese HQ
irving-tx.local_fact: Irving's Las Colinas was ExxonMobil's corporate home for 34 years until 2023 — today Kimberly-Clark, McKesson, Celanese, and Fluor anchor the Fortune 500 presence in one of Texas's densest corporate campus corridors
irving-tx.commute_note: DFW International Airport sits on the city's northwest border, feeding Irving with heavy commuter and airport-service traffic
irving-tx.price_factor: ExxonMobil-branded stations are common but rarely the cheapest; Costco Irving and Sam's Club drive discount prices near the airport

laredo-tx.major_employer: Customs and Border Protection, trade/logistics firms, Doctors Hospital of Laredo
laredo-tx.local_fact: Laredo handles 3.35 million commercial trucks per year — the busiest US land port — processing roughly $800 million in daily US-Mexico trade and about 60% of all Texas-Mexico truck freight
laredo-tx.commute_note: I-35's southern terminus is at the World Trade Bridge here; local truck traffic can exceed 14,000 vehicles per day on Mines Road alone
laredo-tx.price_factor: Heavy diesel demand from border freight + cross-border fuel arbitrage with Mexico keeps Laredo prices slightly below the Texas average

garland-tx.major_employer: Kraft Heinz, Raytheon, Resistol Hats, Atlas Copco
garland-tx.local_fact: Garland has one of the largest manufacturing bases in DFW with Kraft Heinz, Raytheon, and Atlas Copco plants employing 20,000+ workers in industrial zones along I-30 and SH-66
garland-tx.commute_note: Garland straddles I-635 (LBJ) and commuters primarily travel west toward downtown Dallas via Garland Road and US-80
garland-tx.price_factor: Manufacturing-area Sam's Club and Murphy USA locations along I-30 frequently post the cheapest gas prices in eastern DFW

frisco-tx.major_employer: Toyota North America (nearby Plano), FC Dallas, Dallas Cowboys practice HQ (The Star), PGA of America HQ
frisco-tx.local_fact: Frisco was the fastest-growing US city over the 2010s and is home to 5 professional sports facilities including the Cowboys' The Star, FC Dallas, and the new PGA of America HQ
frisco-tx.commute_note: Most Frisco commuters drive south on the Dallas North Tollway into Legacy West (Plano) or downtown Dallas
frisco-tx.price_factor: Affluent suburb: branded chain prices average 4-7¢ above the metro mean, with Costco Frisco and Sam's Club providing the main discount anchors

amarillo-tx.major_employer: Tyson Foods, JBS beef plant, Bell Helicopter Amarillo, Pantex nuclear facility
amarillo-tx.local_fact: Amarillo is the cattle-feeding capital of North America, handling ~28% of US cattle feeding within a 100-mile radius, and sits on the world's largest helium reserve at the Cliffside Gas Field
amarillo-tx.commute_note: I-40 is the main coast-to-coast corridor (Barstow CA to Wilmington NC) and brings heavy long-haul freight traffic through Amarillo 24/7
amarillo-tx.price_factor: Truck-stop competition on I-40 (Love's, Pilot, TA) keeps diesel cheap; regular gas tracks the West Texas average

grand-prairie-tx.major_employer: Lockheed Martin Grand Prairie, Bell Flight, Poly-America, IKEA regional
grand-prairie-tx.local_fact: Grand Prairie sits between Dallas and Fort Worth and hosts Lockheed Martin's missile and fire-control operations plus Bell Flight's helicopter assembly
grand-prairie-tx.commute_note: Residents split commutes between Dallas (east) and Fort Worth (west) via I-20, SH-360, and I-30
grand-prairie-tx.price_factor: Mid-DFW location with heavy interstate competition keeps prices near the metro average, with QuikTrip and 7-Eleven as the main discount anchors

mckinney-tx.major_employer: Raytheon McKinney, Emerson Process Management, SRS Distribution, Globe Life
mckinney-tx.local_fact: McKinney is one of the fastest-growing US cities and hosts Raytheon's McKinney campus along with Globe Life insurance and SRS Distribution HQ
mckinney-tx.commute_note: McKinney commuters drive US-75 south through Allen and Plano toward north Dallas; morning US-75 congestion is among the worst in DFW
mckinney-tx.price_factor: Far-north suburb: prices trend 3-6¢ above the metro average, with Costco McKinney and Sam's Club as the main discount anchors

brownsville-tx.major_employer: SpaceX Starbase, Port of Brownsville, Valley Baptist Medical Center, CBP
brownsville-tx.local_fact: SpaceX's Starbase facility at Boca Chica sits 22 miles east by road from Brownsville — the southernmost major US city and home to the Port of Brownsville's steel and petroleum imports
brownsville-tx.commute_note: Brownsville borders Matamoros, Mexico; daily cross-border commuter and commercial traffic flows through 4 international bridges
brownsville-tx.price_factor: Cross-border fuel arbitrage and proximity to the Port of Brownsville's petroleum docks keep prices slightly below the Texas average

killeen-tx.military_base_name: Fort Cavazos (formerly Fort Hood) — 340 square miles (218,000 acres), 36,500 active-duty soldiers
killeen-tx.university_name: Central Texas College, Texas A&M Central Texas
killeen-tx.major_employer: Fort Cavazos (Fort Hood), Seton Medical Center, AdventHealth Central Texas
killeen-tx.local_fact: Killeen sits adjacent to Fort Cavazos — one of the largest US Army installations at 340 square miles (218,000 acres) — home to roughly 36,500 active-duty soldiers plus families
killeen-tx.commute_note: Fort Cavazos gates generate massive morning/evening rush surges on US-190 and Clear Creek Road; residents often drive larger vehicles given military service profiles
killeen-tx.price_factor: High truck ownership and military commuter base drive volume; limited refinery access (~180 miles) keeps prices near state average

pasadena-tx.refinery_name: Shell Deer Park — 340,000 bbl/day, Pasadena Refining 100,000 bbl/day
pasadena-tx.major_employer: Shell Deer Park, LyondellBasell, Pasadena Refining, Port Houston
pasadena-tx.local_fact: Pasadena sits inside the Houston Ship Channel petrochemical corridor — Shell Deer Park, LyondellBasell, and Pasadena Refining operate within the city limits, employing thousands
pasadena-tx.commute_note: SH-225 (La Porte Freeway) carries dense industrial and tanker-truck traffic connecting the Ship Channel refineries to distribution
pasadena-tx.price_factor: Direct refinery proximity: Pasadena typically has among the lowest gas prices in the Houston metro and often in Texas

mesquite-tx.major_employer: Mesquite ISD, Pepsi distribution, ALDI distribution center
mesquite-tx.local_fact: Mesquite hosts the iconic Mesquite Championship Rodeo and sits on the east side of the Dallas metro along I-635 and US-80 corridors
mesquite-tx.commute_note: Most Mesquite commuters drive west into downtown Dallas via I-30 or US-80, both prone to congestion
mesquite-tx.price_factor: East-side Dallas location with standard DFW distribution pricing; Costco Mesquite and Sam's Club anchor discount competition

mcallen-tx.university_name: UT Rio Grande Valley (Edinburg main campus nearby)
mcallen-tx.major_employer: DHR Health, McAllen Medical Center, H-E-B distribution, CBP
mcallen-tx.local_fact: McAllen is the retail and healthcare hub of the Rio Grande Valley — one of the fastest-growing US metros — with heavy cross-border shopping from Reynosa, Mexico
mcallen-tx.commute_note: US-83 (the 'Valley Freeway') runs west-east through McAllen connecting Mission, Pharr, Weslaco, and Harlingen in a continuous commuter belt
mcallen-tx.price_factor: Cross-border competition with Mexico + low regional wages drive prices slightly below the Texas state average

denton-tx.university_name: University of North Texas (47,000), Texas Woman's University
denton-tx.major_employer: University of North Texas, Peterbilt Motors, Tetra Pak, Texas Health Presbyterian
denton-tx.local_fact: Denton is where I-35 splits into I-35E (Dallas) and I-35W (Fort Worth) and hosts the University of North Texas with ~47,000 students plus Peterbilt Motors truck assembly
denton-tx.commute_note: Denton commuters split between Dallas (east) and Fort Worth (south) with I-35E/35W traffic among the heaviest in DFW
denton-tx.price_factor: I-35 truck-stop competition keeps prices near the metro average; Costco Denton drives discount competition

waco-tx.major_employer: Baylor University, Baylor Scott & White Hillcrest, L3Harris Waco, Magnolia Market (Chip and Joanna Gaines)
waco-tx.local_fact: Waco sits at the I-35 midpoint between San Antonio and Dallas and draws 1.5+ million visitors annually to Magnolia Market — Chip and Joanna Gaines's flagship — plus Baylor University's 21,000 students
waco-tx.commute_note: I-35 through Waco carries continuous NAFTA freight between Mexico and the upper Midwest; congestion spikes during Baylor game days
waco-tx.price_factor: Midpoint on I-35 with heavy truck-stop competition: Waco prices typically track the state average within 1-2¢

carrollton-tx.major_employer: Halliburton branch offices, Amerisource Bergen, Luminant (Vistra), McKesson operations
carrollton-tx.local_fact: Carrollton sits at the intersection of I-35E and the President George Bush Turnpike (SH-190), with one of the largest Korean-American communities in the southern US
carrollton-tx.commute_note: Carrollton commuters use I-35E and the Dallas North Tollway to reach Legacy West (Plano) and downtown Dallas
carrollton-tx.price_factor: Central DFW location with standard metro pricing; Sam's Club Carrollton and Costco Lewisville (nearby) drive discount competition

midland-tx.major_employer: ExxonMobil (acquired Pioneer 2024), ConocoPhillips (acquired Concho 2021), ExxonMobil Permian, oilfield services firms
midland-tx.local_fact: Midland is the business capital of the Permian Basin — the most productive oil field in the US — with 29+ active rigs in Midland County alone and crude traveling 300 miles to Gulf refineries then back as finished product
midland-tx.commute_note: Oilfield traffic dominates I-20 and SH-349 with heavy truck flow between Midland and Odessa 24/7; local pickup truck ownership is among the highest in Texas
midland-tx.price_factor: Despite massive local crude production, prices run 5-10¢ above the state average — finished fuel must travel 600 miles round-trip from Gulf refineries

pearland-tx.major_employer: Memorial Hermann, Kelsey-Seybold Clinic, HCA Houston Healthcare, Texas Medical Center commuters
pearland-tx.local_fact: Pearland was Houston's fastest-growing suburb in the 2010s — SH-288 now provides a direct 20-minute drive to the Texas Medical Center, Pearland's single largest employer via commuters
pearland-tx.commute_note: Morning SH-288 rush is dense with TMC-bound medical commuters; Beltway 8 adds cross-metro truck traffic
pearland-tx.price_factor: Houston-metro pricing: refinery proximity (~30 miles to Texas City and Deer Park) keeps Pearland gas near the metro average

abilene-tx.military_base_name: Dyess Air Force Base (B-1 Lancer bombers, C-130J transports)
abilene-tx.university_name: Abilene Christian University, Hardin-Simmons University, McMurry University
abilene-tx.major_employer: Dyess AFB, Hendrick Medical Center, Abilene Christian University, cotton/cattle ranches
abilene-tx.local_fact: Abilene is home to Dyess Air Force Base — the only B-1 Lancer bomber base in the US — plus three Christian universities (ACU, Hardin-Simmons, McMurry) and a strong cattle/cotton economy
abilene-tx.commute_note: I-20 carries Dallas-to-El Paso long-haul freight through Abilene; short local commutes (~16 minutes average) keep resident fuel usage moderate
abilene-tx.price_factor: Truck-stop competition on I-20 plus lower West Texas distribution costs typically keep Abilene prices at or just below the state average

college-station-tx.major_employer: Texas A&M University, Texas A&M Health Science Center, Reynolds and Reynolds, Sanderson Farms
college-station-tx.local_fact: College Station is home to Texas A&M University — 74,000 students, the 12th Man tradition, and Kyle Field with 102,000-capacity stadium — one of the largest college football venues in the US
college-station-tx.commute_note: Traffic surges on SH-6 for Aggie home football games; weekday commuting is light with short student trips dominating
college-station-tx.price_factor: Student-heavy demand and limited chain competition keep CSTAT prices 2-4¢ above the state average outside summer break

richardson-tx.university_name: University of Texas at Dallas (31,000 students)
richardson-tx.major_employer: Texas Instruments HQ, AT&T, Blue Cross Blue Shield, Fujitsu, UT Dallas
richardson-tx.local_fact: Richardson's 'Telecom Corridor' hosts Texas Instruments' corporate HQ along with AT&T, Fujitsu, and Blue Cross Blue Shield — one of the largest tech employer clusters in Texas
richardson-tx.commute_note: US-75 Central Expressway is the main commute artery, feeding downtown Dallas; congestion is heavy both directions
richardson-tx.price_factor: Central DFW location with standard metro pricing; Costco Plano and Sam's Club Richardson drive discount competition

round-rock-tx.major_employer: Dell Technologies HQ, Emerson Automation, St. David's Round Rock
round-rock-tx.local_fact: Round Rock hosts Dell Technologies' corporate HQ — the city's single largest employer — and serves as the northern anchor of the Austin tech corridor
round-rock-tx.commute_note: Round Rock commuters flood I-35 south into Austin each morning; I-35 through Round Rock is one of the most congested stretches in Texas
round-rock-tx.price_factor: Austin-adjacent pricing trends 3-5¢ above the state average; Sam's Club Round Rock and Costco Cedar Park drive discount competition

odessa-tx.major_employer: ExxonMobil Permian (acquired Pioneer 2024), Halliburton, Schlumberger, oilfield services
odessa-tx.local_fact: Odessa is the blue-collar twin of Midland in the Permian Basin — setting for Friday Night Lights — with 26+ active rigs in Ector County and ExxonMobil now the dominant operator after its 2024 Pioneer acquisition
odessa-tx.commute_note: Oilfield pickup trucks dominate SH-191 and I-20 between Odessa and Midland, with daily crew-change traffic peaking early morning
odessa-tx.price_factor: Like Midland, Odessa pays above-average prices despite crude production — finished gasoline must travel from Gulf refineries 300 miles away

league-city-tx.refinery_name: Texas City refineries cluster (Valero, Marathon, etc.) ~15 miles
league-city-tx.major_employer: NASA Johnson Space Center (nearby), Boeing, Lockheed Martin Aerospace, UTMB
league-city-tx.local_fact: League City sits 8 miles from NASA Johnson Space Center and is the largest city in Galveston County — the Clear Lake aerospace corridor is its primary employer base
league-city-tx.commute_note: I-45 south from Houston to Galveston passes through League City with heavy Houston-bound commuter traffic each morning
league-city-tx.price_factor: Very close to Texas City refinery cluster; prices track among the lowest in the Houston metro

beaumont-tx.refinery_name: ExxonMobil Beaumont — 369,000 bbl/day, plus nearby Motiva Port Arthur
beaumont-tx.major_employer: ExxonMobil Beaumont Refinery, Motiva (Port Arthur), DuPont, Lamar University
beaumont-tx.local_fact: Beaumont is near the Spindletop gusher site where the modern Texas oil industry began on January 10, 1901 — today ExxonMobil Beaumont (369K bbl/day) and the Port Arthur Motiva refinery (17 miles away) anchor the economy
beaumont-tx.commute_note: I-10 through Beaumont is the main Houston-to-New Orleans corridor; heavy tanker and chemical freight runs 24/7
beaumont-tx.price_factor: Direct refinery proximity and petrochemical-worker volume make Beaumont one of the cheapest gas markets in Texas

lewisville-tx.major_employer: Lewisville ISD, Xerox, Nationwide Insurance, McKesson regional offices
lewisville-tx.local_fact: Lewisville sits at the I-35E/SH-121 intersection and is home to the massive Lewisville Lake, one of the most-visited lakes in Texas, plus a growing corporate campus corridor along SH-121
lewisville-tx.commute_note: I-35E runs through Lewisville connecting Denton to Dallas; Lake Lewisville draws weekend recreation traffic
lewisville-tx.price_factor: Costco Lewisville and nearby Sam's Club are among the cheapest fuel stops in north DFW

sugar-land-tx.major_employer: Fluor Corporation HQ, Schlumberger, Noble Energy, CVR Energy
sugar-land-tx.local_fact: Sugar Land hosts Fluor Corporation's HQ — named after the historic Imperial Sugar refinery site — and anchors wealthy Fort Bend County with one of the most diverse populations in Texas
sugar-land-tx.commute_note: US-59 (I-69) carries Sugar Land commuters into downtown Houston with heavy morning congestion; Beltway 8 provides the main loop alternative
sugar-land-tx.price_factor: Houston-metro pricing with slight upscale-suburb premium; Costco Sugar Land and Sam's Club provide discount anchors

allen-tx.major_employer: Experian, IBM, Cadence, Allen Premium Outlets, Credit Union of Texas
allen-tx.local_fact: Allen is anchored by Allen Premium Outlets (one of the largest outlet malls in Texas) and hosts major offices for Experian, IBM, and Cadence Design Systems along the US-75 corridor
allen-tx.commute_note: Allen commuters drive south on US-75 through Plano into north Dallas; traffic builds early with Plano and Richardson tech workers
allen-tx.price_factor: Affluent north-DFW suburb with prices 4-6¢ above metro average; Costco Frisco and Sam's Club drive local discount competition

tyler-tx.university_name: UT Tyler, UT Health Science Center Tyler
tyler-tx.major_employer: UT Health East Texas, CHRISTUS Trinity Mother Frances, Brookshire Grocery Company HQ
tyler-tx.local_fact: Tyler is the 'Rose Capital of America' and the hub of East Texas — the 1930s East Texas Oil Field once produced more crude than any field on Earth, and UT Health Tyler anchors the modern economy
tyler-tx.commute_note: Tyler sits 35 miles off I-20 on US-69; Loop 323 carries most local commuter traffic
tyler-tx.price_factor: East Texas location with moderate distribution costs; Sam's Club Tyler and Brookshire's drive competitive pricing

wichita-falls-tx.military_base_name: Sheppard Air Force Base — ~5,973 active-duty (NATO pilot training, technical training)
wichita-falls-tx.major_employer: Sheppard AFB, United Regional Health Care, Midwestern State University, Vitro Flat Glass
wichita-falls-tx.local_fact: Wichita Falls hosts Sheppard Air Force Base — the Air Force's primary pilot training center with approximately 5,973 active-duty personnel and one of only two Euro-NATO Joint Jet Pilot Training (ENJJPT) programs in the world
wichita-falls-tx.commute_note: US-287 connects Wichita Falls northwest to Amarillo and southeast to Fort Worth; cross-state freight uses this route
wichita-falls-tx.price_factor: Far-north Texas location with Oklahoma fuel-market competition just across the Red River keeps prices near or below state average

edinburg-tx.university_name: UT Rio Grande Valley (main campus, 32,000 students)
edinburg-tx.major_employer: UT Rio Grande Valley, Hidalgo County government, Doctors Hospital at Renaissance
edinburg-tx.local_fact: Edinburg hosts the main campus of UT Rio Grande Valley with 32,000 students and is the seat of Hidalgo County government — the Valley's administrative center
edinburg-tx.commute_note: US-281 runs north-south through Edinburg connecting McAllen to the Pharr-Reynosa International Bridge
edinburg-tx.price_factor: Rio Grande Valley pricing with cross-border arbitrage tends to run 2-4¢ below state average

san-angelo-tx.military_base_name: Goodfellow Air Force Base (intelligence and firefighter training)
san-angelo-tx.major_employer: Goodfellow AFB, Shannon Medical Center, Angelo State University, cattle/sheep ranching
san-angelo-tx.local_fact: San Angelo is the wool and mohair capital of the US and sits 170 miles from the nearest interstate (I-20 or I-10) — one of the most remote major cities in Texas — anchored by Goodfellow AFB's intelligence school
san-angelo-tx.commute_note: With no interstate access, all freight and commuter traffic uses US-87 and US-67 — local commutes are short but regional trips require extensive highway driving
san-angelo-tx.price_factor: Remote location and long trucking distances from Permian Basin terminals push prices 8-12¢ above state average in most weeks

conroe-tx.major_employer: Conroe ISD, HCA Houston Healthcare Conroe, McKesson distribution, Anadarko (Woodlands-adjacent)
conroe-tx.local_fact: Conroe is Montgomery County's fastest-growing city — Lake Conroe draws 3+ million recreation visitors annually, and I-45 connects residents to The Woodlands and north Houston in 30 minutes
conroe-tx.commute_note: I-45 is the main artery; morning southbound traffic to The Woodlands and downtown Houston is heavy
conroe-tx.price_factor: North Houston metro pricing with refinery access via distribution networks keeps Conroe prices near the metro average

new-braunfels-tx.major_employer: Caterpillar manufacturing, Schlitterbahn Waterpark, Resolute Health Hospital, Wurstfest tourism
new-braunfels-tx.local_fact: New Braunfels sits on the I-35 corridor between Austin and San Antonio and is home to Schlitterbahn Waterpark and the Comal and Guadalupe rivers — drawing over 4 million tubing visitors each summer
new-braunfels-tx.commute_note: Split commutes: some drive south to San Antonio, others north to Austin via I-35; tourism traffic on SH-46 and I-35 peaks April-September
new-braunfels-tx.price_factor: Austin-SA midpoint with I-35 truck-stop competition keeps prices near the state average

atascocita-tx.major_employer: Humble ISD (local schools), Kingwood Medical Center, Memorial Hermann Northeast
atascocita-tx.local_fact: Atascocita is an unincorporated community on the north shore of Lake Houston — one of the fastest-growing areas in Harris County — with most residents commuting into Houston via US-59
atascocita-tx.commute_note: US-59 southbound to downtown Houston is a 35-40 minute morning drive; Beltway 8 provides the main cross-metro alternative
atascocita-tx.price_factor: North Houston metro with access to Ship Channel refineries via distribution keeps prices near the metro average

bryan-tx.university_name: Blinn College (main campus), Texas A&M adjacent
bryan-tx.major_employer: Sanderson Farms (Wayne-Sanderson), St. Joseph Health, Blinn College, Reynolds and Reynolds
bryan-tx.local_fact: Bryan is the twin city of College Station and home to the original Texas A&M-related economy — Sanderson Farms poultry and Blinn College anchor the working-class side of the Brazos Valley
bryan-tx.commute_note: Most workers commute to College Station or within Bryan; SH-6 is the main north-south artery
bryan-tx.price_factor: Slightly cheaper than College Station with more chain competition and less student-driven demand

mission-tx.major_employer: Mission Regional Medical Center, Mission CISD, agricultural packing, CBP
mission-tx.local_fact: Mission is home to the Texas Citrus Fiesta and is part of the continuous McAllen-Edinburg-Mission MSA — one of the fastest-growing metro areas in the US
mission-tx.commute_note: US-83 runs through Mission connecting it to McAllen and Pharr in the Valley's commuter belt
mission-tx.price_factor: Rio Grande Valley pricing — 2-4¢ below state average due to cross-border competition with Mexico

baytown-tx.refinery_name: ExxonMobil Baytown — 588,000 bbl/day (third-largest in US as of 2025)
baytown-tx.major_employer: ExxonMobil Baytown (7,000 employees), Chevron Phillips Chemical, Covestro
baytown-tx.local_fact: ExxonMobil's Baytown Refinery runs 588,000 barrels per day — the third-largest refinery in the United States as of 2025 after Motiva Port Arthur took the top spot — employing 7,000 workers on a 3,400-acre complex, and it's the single largest employer in Baytown
baytown-tx.commute_note: I-10 and SH-146 carry dense petrochemical tanker and worker traffic with shift changes at Exxon creating predictable rush patterns
baytown-tx.price_factor: Direct refinery adjacency — Baytown regularly has some of the cheapest gas prices in the entire state

temple-tx.major_employer: Baylor Scott & White Health (HQ + flagship hospital), McLane Company HQ, Scott & White Clinic
temple-tx.local_fact: Temple is the headquarters of Baylor Scott & White Health — the largest not-for-profit health system in Texas — and hosts McLane Company HQ, the largest US distributor of food to convenience stores
temple-tx.commute_note: I-35 through Temple is a heavy NAFTA freight corridor with daily Dallas-to-San Antonio truck flow
temple-tx.price_factor: I-35 truck-stop pricing pressure plus McLane distribution network keeps Temple at or slightly below the state average

longview-tx.major_employer: Eastman Chemical, Komatsu Mining, CHRISTUS Good Shepherd, LeTourneau University
longview-tx.local_fact: Longview sits atop the remnants of the East Texas Oil Field (the largest US oil discovery until Prudhoe Bay) — Eastman Chemical's Longview site is one of the largest chemical plants in Texas
longview-tx.commute_note: I-20 carries continuous Dallas-to-Shreveport freight through Longview; US-259 connects to Texarkana
longview-tx.price_factor: East Texas discount pricing with strong Sam's Club/Walmart competition often makes Longview one of the cheapest major TX markets

pharr-tx.major_employer: Pharr-Reynosa International Bridge operations, warehousing/logistics firms, CBP
pharr-tx.local_fact: The Pharr-Reynosa International Bridge is the second-busiest commercial crossing between Texas and Mexico and handles the majority of US produce imports from Mexico
pharr-tx.commute_note: US-281 runs south from Pharr to the bridge; heavy commercial truck traffic dominates 24/7
pharr-tx.price_factor: Diesel demand from cross-border produce freight is enormous; regular gas runs 2-4¢ below state average

flower-mound-tx.major_employer: Stryker Communications, Centurion Medical Products, Cytracom, local government/schools
flower-mound-tx.local_fact: Flower Mound is one of DFW's wealthiest suburbs — sitting on the shores of Grapevine Lake with direct access to DFW Airport (10 minutes) and Legacy West (15 minutes)
flower-mound-tx.commute_note: Residents commute primarily via SH-121 to Legacy West/Plano or south to DFW Airport; no heavy truck freight
flower-mound-tx.price_factor: High-income suburb: brand prices run 4-7¢ above metro average; QuikTrip and Costco Flower Mound drive discount pricing

cedar-park-tx.major_employer: Apple campus (15 miles south), Dell (Round Rock adjacent), Firefly Aerospace, HEB Plus
cedar-park-tx.local_fact: Cedar Park is the fastest-growing city in Williamson County with Apple's $1B Austin campus 15 miles south and Firefly Aerospace's rocket development HQ in nearby Briggs
cedar-park-tx.commute_note: US-183 is the main commuter artery south into north Austin and the Domain tech corridor
cedar-park-tx.price_factor: Austin-adjacent pricing trends 3-5¢ above state average; Costco Cedar Park drives discount competition

mansfield-tx.major_employer: Mouser Electronics HQ, Klein Tools, Mansfield ISD, Methodist Mansfield Medical Center
mansfield-tx.local_fact: Mansfield hosts Mouser Electronics' global HQ — one of the world's largest electronic components distributors — and Klein Tools' headquarters, making it a small but concentrated DFW manufacturing hub
mansfield-tx.commute_note: US-287 and SH-360 connect Mansfield to Arlington, Fort Worth, and south Dallas
mansfield-tx.price_factor: South DFW pricing with standard metro distribution; Sam's Club Mansfield drives local discount competition

georgetown-tx.major_employer: Sun City Texas retirement community, St. David's Georgetown Hospital, Southwestern University, Citigroup
georgetown-tx.local_fact: Georgetown was the fastest-growing US city in the 2021-2022 Census period, driven by the 10,000+ resident Sun City Texas retirement community — now the largest active-adult community in Texas
georgetown-tx.commute_note: I-35 carries Georgetown commuters south into Austin; SH-130 provides a tolled alternative around Austin congestion
georgetown-tx.price_factor: Austin-metro pricing trends with limited refinery access and affluent retirement demographics push prices 3-5¢ above state average

missouri-city-tx.major_employer: Fort Bend ISD, local healthcare, commuter workforce to Houston
missouri-city-tx.local_fact: Missouri City is part of the ethnically diverse Fort Bend County and is home to Sienna — one of the largest master-planned communities in Texas
missouri-city-tx.commute_note: Residents commute into downtown Houston and the Texas Medical Center via US-59/I-69 and Beltway 8
missouri-city-tx.price_factor: Houston-metro pricing with refinery-network access keeps Missouri City near the metro average

harlingen-tx.major_employer: Valley Baptist Medical Center, Harlingen CISD, Marine Military Academy, CBP Border Patrol sector HQ
harlingen-tx.local_fact: Harlingen is the Rio Grande Valley's healthcare and transportation hub — home to the Valley International Airport and the US Border Patrol's RGV Sector HQ
harlingen-tx.commute_note: US-77/I-69E connects Harlingen to Brownsville south and Corpus Christi north with heavy commercial freight
harlingen-tx.price_factor: Valley pricing with cross-border arbitrage keeps Harlingen 2-4¢ below state average

north-richland-hills-tx.major_employer: Healthmark Industries, Santander Consumer USA, NRH Parks and Recreation, retail
north-richland-hills-tx.local_fact: North Richland Hills is a mid-cities suburb between Fort Worth and DFW Airport, known for NRH2O Family Water Park and proximity to the AllianceTexas logistics corridor
north-richland-hills-tx.commute_note: Loop 820 and SH-183 carry commuters between Fort Worth (south) and DFW Airport (east)
north-richland-hills-tx.price_factor: Standard DFW mid-cities pricing; Sam's Club and QuikTrip drive discount competition

rowlett-tx.major_employer: Rowlett ISD, Dallas commuter workforce, Lake Ray Hubbard tourism
rowlett-tx.local_fact: Rowlett sits on the shores of Lake Ray Hubbard — a major Dallas-area reservoir — and has grown as a bedroom community for northeast Dallas commuters
rowlett-tx.commute_note: I-30 west into Dallas is the primary commute route; morning congestion is moderate compared to other DFW corridors
rowlett-tx.price_factor: Northeast Dallas suburb with standard metro pricing; limited large warehouse stores make QuikTrip and 7-Eleven the main price setters

san-marcos-tx.major_employer: Texas State University, San Marcos Premium Outlets, Tanger Outlets
san-marcos-tx.local_fact: San Marcos hosts Texas State University (38,000 students) and two massive outlet malls — Premium and Tanger — that draw millions of shoppers from Austin, San Antonio, and Mexico
san-marcos-tx.commute_note: I-35 splits commuter flows: north to Austin (34 miles), south to San Antonio (48 miles); outlet-mall traffic spikes weekends
san-marcos-tx.price_factor: I-35 truck-stop competition and outlet-mall travel volume keep prices near or slightly below state average

leander-tx.major_employer: Austin tech commuters, Leander ISD, Lakeline Mall retail corridor
leander-tx.local_fact: Leander has been one of the fastest-growing small cities in the US for over a decade, anchored by Cedar Park and north Austin tech commuters seeking affordable housing
leander-tx.commute_note: US-183 south through Cedar Park feeds Leander commuters into the Domain and downtown Austin
leander-tx.price_factor: Austin-metro pricing with limited large chains — residents often drive to Cedar Park Costco or Round Rock Sam's for discount fuel

victoria-tx.major_employer: Caterpillar Victoria plant, DuPont Victoria, Formosa Plastics Point Comfort (nearby), Citizens Medical Center
victoria-tx.local_fact: Victoria sits at the crossroads of US-59 (I-69) and US-77 — the main freight corridors between Houston, San Antonio, and the Rio Grande Valley — and hosts a major Caterpillar hydraulic excavator plant
victoria-tx.commute_note: US-59 between Houston and the Valley funnels heavy truck traffic through Victoria 24/7
victoria-tx.price_factor: Corpus Christi refinery access (60 miles) and US-59 freight competition keep Victoria prices near or below state average

pflugerville-tx.major_employer: Samsung Austin Semiconductor (nearby Taylor), Austin tech commuters, Pflugerville ISD
pflugerville-tx.local_fact: Pflugerville is adjacent to Samsung's $17 billion semiconductor plant in Taylor — one of the largest foreign direct investments in Texas history — and serves as a north-Austin tech bedroom community
pflugerville-tx.commute_note: SH-130 toll road and I-35 carry Pflugerville commuters south into Austin's tech corridor
pflugerville-tx.price_factor: Austin-metro pricing — Costco Cedar Park and Sam's Club Round Rock are the nearest warehouse discount options

spring-tx.major_employer: ExxonMobil Houston Campus (15,000 employees, Spring), HPE, Southwestern Energy
spring-tx.local_fact: Spring is home to ExxonMobil's Houston Campus — a 385-acre facility with 15,000 employees, one of the largest corporate campuses in Texas — plus HPE's Houston headquarters
spring-tx.commute_note: I-45 south into downtown Houston is dense every weekday; The Woodlands and ExxonMobil campus traffic compounds the Spring section
spring-tx.price_factor: North Houston metro with refinery distribution network access — prices track the metro average

euless-tx.major_employer: DFW International Airport workforce, Bell Flight nearby, Texas Health Harris Methodist
euless-tx.local_fact: Euless sits directly south of DFW International Airport and has one of the largest Tongan-American communities in the US
euless-tx.commute_note: SH-183 and SH-360 feed DFW Airport — one of the world's 5 busiest — creating constant shift-change traffic for 60,000+ airport workers
euless-tx.price_factor: Airport-adjacent location with high volume keeps competition tight; standard DFW mid-cities pricing

wylie-tx.major_employer: Sanden International, Extruders Inc, Wylie ISD
wylie-tx.local_fact: Wylie was historically a small agricultural town that has grown as a Collin County bedroom community — now home to Sanden International's US HQ for automotive air-conditioning systems
wylie-tx.commute_note: Residents commute west via SH-78 toward Plano and Richardson tech corridors
wylie-tx.price_factor: Distant north-Collin suburb with modest chain competition; prices track the metro average

kyle-tx.major_employer: Seton Medical Center Hays, Ascension Seton, Austin tech commuter workforce
kyle-tx.local_fact: Kyle is one of the fastest-growing cities in Hays County and sits directly on I-35 — about 20 miles south of downtown Austin — making it a popular Austin bedroom community
kyle-tx.commute_note: I-35 northbound to Austin is heavily congested every weekday morning
kyle-tx.price_factor: Austin-metro pricing — residents often drive to nearby San Marcos outlets-area fuel or Buda H-E-B for discount

desoto-tx.major_employer: UPS South Dallas hub, Amazon fulfillment, FedEx SmartPost, Baylor Scott & White DeSoto
desoto-tx.local_fact: DeSoto sits along the I-35E / US-67 corridor south of Dallas and hosts major UPS, Amazon, and FedEx distribution facilities — part of the Best Southwest logistics cluster
desoto-tx.commute_note: I-35E north to downtown Dallas is a 25-minute commute with moderate morning congestion
desoto-tx.price_factor: South Dallas logistics corridor with heavy diesel demand; regular gas tracks the metro average

port-arthur-tx.refinery_name: Motiva Port Arthur — 730,000 bbl/day (largest in US as of 2025), plus Valero Port Arthur 435,000 bbl/day
port-arthur-tx.major_employer: Motiva (Shell/Aramco) refinery, Valero Port Arthur, TotalEnergies, Golden Pass LNG
port-arthur-tx.local_fact: Port Arthur hosts the Motiva Port Arthur Refinery — the largest refinery in the United States at 730,000 bbl/day as of 2025 — along with Valero Port Arthur (435,000 bbl/day) and TotalEnergies refineries nearby
port-arthur-tx.commute_note: Refinery shift-change traffic dominates US-69 and SH-73 daily; tanker-truck volume is among the highest in the state
port-arthur-tx.price_factor: Refinery-cluster direct access — Port Arthur typically has the very lowest gas prices in Texas

galveston-tx.major_employer: UTMB Galveston, Port of Galveston cruise terminal, tourism, Carnival/Royal Caribbean cruise operations
galveston-tx.local_fact: Galveston is one of the busiest US cruise ports (2M+ passengers/year) and hosts UTMB — the oldest medical school in Texas — plus historic Pleasure Pier and Moody Gardens
galveston-tx.commute_note: I-45 is the lone artery connecting Galveston Island to mainland Texas; causeway traffic peaks summer weekends
galveston-tx.price_factor: Island location adds modest transport cost, but Texas City refinery proximity keeps Galveston near the Houston metro average

texas-city-tx.refinery_name: Marathon Galveston Bay — 631,000 bbl/day, Valero Texas City 245,000 bbl/day
texas-city-tx.major_employer: Marathon Galveston Bay Refinery, Valero Texas City, Eastman, ISP Chemicals, Port of Texas City
texas-city-tx.local_fact: Texas City hosts Marathon's Galveston Bay Refinery (631,000 bbl/day) — one of the three largest in the US — plus Valero Texas City, making the city a top-3 refinery cluster alongside Baytown and Port Arthur
texas-city-tx.commute_note: I-45 and SH-146 carry dense refinery worker and tanker-truck traffic daily
texas-city-tx.price_factor: One of the top refinery clusters in the US — Texas City consistently posts some of the cheapest gas prices in the state

burleson-tx.major_employer: Huguley Health System, Centennial High School / BISD, Fort Worth commuter workforce
burleson-tx.local_fact: Burleson sits on I-35W about 15 miles south of downtown Fort Worth and is a fast-growing bedroom community with Johnson County ranching roots
burleson-tx.commute_note: I-35W north into Fort Worth is the primary commute with moderate morning congestion
burleson-tx.price_factor: South Fort Worth suburb; prices track the metro average with Sam's Club Fort Worth as the main discount anchor

grapevine-tx.major_employer: GameStop HQ, Great Wolf Lodge, Gaylord Texan Resort, DFW Airport adjacent
grapevine-tx.local_fact: Grapevine sits on the north shore of Grapevine Lake adjacent to DFW Airport and hosts Gaylord Texan Resort — one of the largest convention hotels in Texas — plus GameStop's corporate HQ
grapevine-tx.commute_note: SH-114 and SH-121 feed DFW Airport; Grapevine Main Street is a major tourist destination with seasonal traffic spikes
grapevine-tx.price_factor: Airport-adjacent location with tourist premium; branded-chain prices run 3-5¢ above metro average

little-elm-tx.major_employer: Little Elm ISD, Frisco commuter workforce, retail
little-elm-tx.local_fact: Little Elm sits on the shores of Lake Lewisville in the Frisco-adjacent boom belt — one of the fastest-growing small cities in north Texas
little-elm-tx.commute_note: US-380 carries heavy east-west commuter traffic between Frisco, Denton, and McKinney
little-elm-tx.price_factor: North Collin pricing trends 4-6¢ above metro average; local Costco Frisco and Sam's Club drive discount competition

cedar-hill-tx.major_employer: Cedar Hill State Park, Hillside Village mall, Cedar Hill ISD, Dallas commuter workforce
cedar-hill-tx.local_fact: Cedar Hill State Park on Joe Pool Lake draws 500,000+ visitors annually, and the Cedar Hill 'Tower Hills' host TV/radio broadcast towers at some of the highest elevations in North Texas
cedar-hill-tx.commute_note: US-67 carries commuters north into downtown Dallas with moderate morning congestion
cedar-hill-tx.price_factor: South DFW pricing with standard metro distribution; Sam's Club Duncanville drives discount competition

bedford-tx.major_employer: Texas Health HEB Hospital, Bell Flight nearby, retail, DFW Airport workforce
bedford-tx.local_fact: Bedford is part of the 'HEB' mid-cities (Hurst-Euless-Bedford) between Fort Worth and DFW Airport — a compact commuter belt along SH-121 and Loop 820
bedford-tx.commute_note: Residents commute to DFW Airport, Fort Worth, or north Dallas via SH-121 and SH-183
bedford-tx.price_factor: Standard DFW mid-cities pricing with strong QuikTrip and RaceTrac competition

rockwall-tx.major_employer: Rockwall ISD, Texas Health Presbyterian Rockwall, Harbor tourism, L-3 Communications
rockwall-tx.local_fact: Rockwall is Texas's smallest county by area and sits on the east shore of Lake Ray Hubbard — The Harbor retail district and lakefront dining draw Dallas weekenders
rockwall-tx.commute_note: I-30 west into downtown Dallas is about 30 minutes with typical morning congestion
rockwall-tx.price_factor: East Dallas exurb with Sam's Club Rockwall and QuikTrip driving competition near metro average

huntsville-tx.major_employer: Sam Houston State University, Texas Department of Criminal Justice HQ (multiple state prisons), HEB distribution
huntsville-tx.local_fact: Huntsville is the headquarters of the Texas Department of Criminal Justice — with 7 state prison units in and around the city — plus Sam Houston State University's 21,000 students
huntsville-tx.commute_note: I-45 is the main Houston-to-Dallas route and brings continuous freight traffic through Huntsville
huntsville-tx.price_factor: I-45 truck-stop competition keeps Huntsville prices near state average; Buc-ee's Madisonville just north provides a regional cheap-fuel destination

keller-tx.major_employer: Keller ISD (the city's largest employer), DFW Airport commuter workforce, retail
keller-tx.local_fact: Keller is among DFW's highest-income suburbs with Keller ISD consistently ranked among the top public districts in Texas
keller-tx.commute_note: Residents commute south to Fort Worth or east to DFW Airport and Alliance corporate parks
keller-tx.price_factor: Affluent north-Fort Worth suburb: branded-chain prices run 4-6¢ above metro average

haltom-city-tx.major_employer: small/medium manufacturing, Fort Worth commuter workforce, auto salvage industry
haltom-city-tx.local_fact: Haltom City is a working-class Fort Worth suburb along Loop 820 with a large concentration of small-to-medium manufacturing and auto-related businesses
haltom-city-tx.commute_note: Loop 820 carries heavy Fort Worth commuter traffic; US-377 connects to downtown Fort Worth
haltom-city-tx.price_factor: Budget-suburb pricing with strong QuikTrip/RaceTrac presence; often among the cheapest mid-cities markets

the-colony-tx.major_employer: Grandscape (Nebraska Furniture Mart), Nebraska Furniture Mart warehouse, Plano/Frisco commuter workforce
the-colony-tx.local_fact: The Colony hosts the Grandscape mixed-use development anchored by Nebraska Furniture Mart's 1.8-million-sq-ft store — the largest furniture store in the US
the-colony-tx.commute_note: SH-121 east carries commuters to Plano and Frisco Legacy tech corridor
the-colony-tx.price_factor: Frisco-adjacent pricing 3-5¢ above metro average; Sam's Club nearby in Lewisville drives discount competition

sherman-tx.major_employer: Texas Instruments semiconductor fab (new $30B facility), GlobiTech semiconductor, Grayson College
sherman-tx.local_fact: Sherman is becoming a major semiconductor manufacturing hub — Texas Instruments is building a $30 billion chip fab, one of the largest private investments in Texas history
sherman-tx.commute_note: US-75 connects Sherman south to McKinney and Dallas — morning traffic has grown significantly with new chip fab construction
sherman-tx.price_factor: Far-north Texas with heavy freight traffic on US-75; prices track state average with Sam's Club Sherman as discount anchor

coppell-tx.major_employer: Amazon fulfillment center (multiple), CEVA Logistics, T-Mobile, Hilton Hotels regional
coppell-tx.local_fact: Coppell is a small affluent suburb adjacent to DFW Airport that hosts one of the densest logistics / fulfillment center clusters in the metroplex — multiple Amazon facilities plus CEVA Logistics
coppell-tx.commute_note: SH-121 feeds DFW Airport and I-635 runs east-west; heavy truck freight flows to/from the airport around the clock
coppell-tx.price_factor: Airport-adjacent with standard DFW pricing; Sam's Club Grapevine and Costco Las Colinas drive competition

schertz-tx.major_employer: Amazon fulfillment (SAT2), Sysco distribution, HEB distribution, Randolph AFB commuter workforce
schertz-tx.local_fact: Schertz sits on I-35 just northeast of San Antonio and hosts one of Amazon's largest Texas fulfillment centers plus Sysco and HEB distribution facilities
schertz-tx.commute_note: I-35 southbound into San Antonio carries continuous commercial freight and commuter traffic
schertz-tx.price_factor: San Antonio metro pricing with heavy logistics diesel demand; regular gas tracks state average

lancaster-tx.major_employer: Amazon MDW2, UPS South Dallas hub, Walmart distribution, Niagara Bottling
lancaster-tx.local_fact: Lancaster is part of the massive Best Southwest inland port — Amazon, UPS, and Walmart operate 1-million-sq-ft distribution facilities along I-35E and I-20
lancaster-tx.commute_note: I-35E and I-20 intersect at Lancaster with among the heaviest truck freight volume in the Dallas metro
lancaster-tx.price_factor: Heavy diesel demand from distribution cluster; regular gas tracks metro average with RaceTrac and QT as discount anchors

friendswood-tx.major_employer: NASA Johnson Space Center nearby (Clear Lake aerospace corridor), Houston Methodist Clear Lake, Friendswood ISD
friendswood-tx.local_fact: Friendswood sits adjacent to NASA's Clear Lake aerospace corridor — home to Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and SpaceX engineers — and is consistently ranked among the best Houston-area school districts
friendswood-tx.commute_note: FM-528 and Beltway 8 feed commuters into the NASA/Clear Lake area and downtown Houston
friendswood-tx.price_factor: Houston metro pricing with proximity to Texas City refineries keeps Friendswood near metro average

duncanville-tx.major_employer: Duncanville ISD, Baylor Scott & White Medical, retail, Dallas commuter workforce
duncanville-tx.local_fact: Duncanville sits on I-20 and US-67 in the Best Southwest commuter belt and is known for one of the most successful public-school basketball programs in Texas
duncanville-tx.commute_note: I-20 east into Dallas is the primary commute; truck freight volume is moderate
duncanville-tx.price_factor: South DFW standard pricing; Sam's Club Duncanville drives discount competition

weslaco-tx.major_employer: Knapp Medical Center, Weslaco ISD, Texas A&M AgriLife citrus research center, agricultural packing
weslaco-tx.local_fact: Weslaco is in the heart of Rio Grande Valley citrus country and hosts the Texas A&M AgriLife Citrus Research Center — the Valley is Texas's primary citrus-producing region
weslaco-tx.commute_note: US-83 connects Weslaco within the continuous Valley commuter corridor from Harlingen to Mission
weslaco-tx.price_factor: Valley cross-border pricing — 2-4¢ below state average

hurst-tx.major_employer: Bell Flight (tiltrotor assembly), NorthPark Mall, DFW Airport commuter workforce
hurst-tx.local_fact: Hurst is part of the 'HEB' mid-cities belt (Hurst-Euless-Bedford) and hosts Bell Flight's V-280 Valor tiltrotor development — the future replacement for Army Black Hawks
hurst-tx.commute_note: Loop 820 through Hurst is one of DFW's busiest — splitting commuter flows between Fort Worth and the airport
hurst-tx.price_factor: Mid-cities standard pricing with strong QuikTrip, RaceTrac, and Sam's Club competition

texarkana-tx.major_employer: Red River Army Depot, Cooper Tire plant, CHRISTUS St. Michael, Wadley Regional
texarkana-tx.local_fact: Texarkana straddles the Texas-Arkansas state line — State Line Avenue literally divides the two cities — and hosts Red River Army Depot, the largest employer in the region
texarkana-tx.commute_note: I-30 carries heavy Dallas-to-Little Rock freight traffic; state-line shoppers cross daily for tax and fuel differences
texarkana-tx.price_factor: Cross-state competition with Arkansas (lower gas tax) pushes Texarkana TX prices to among the state's lowest

copperas-cove-tx.military_base_name: Fort Cavazos (Fort Hood) — primary gate at Copperas Cove
copperas-cove-tx.major_employer: Fort Cavazos (civilian and military jobs), Copperas Cove ISD, Seton Medical Center Harker Heights
copperas-cove-tx.local_fact: Copperas Cove abuts Fort Cavazos (Fort Hood) with the primary west gate at its east edge — one of the two main residential cities for Fort Cavazos soldiers alongside Killeen
copperas-cove-tx.commute_note: US-190 connects Copperas Cove to Killeen and Fort Cavazos gates; traffic surges at military shift changes
copperas-cove-tx.price_factor: Military-town pricing with moderate competition; prices track state average

socorro-tx.major_employer: El Paso commuter workforce, Fort Bliss, Socorro ISD
socorro-tx.local_fact: Socorro sits on the southeast edge of El Paso along the Rio Grande and is one of the oldest continuously inhabited settlements in Texas — founded in 1680
socorro-tx.commute_note: I-10 northwest to El Paso and Fort Bliss carries the majority of Socorro's commuter traffic
socorro-tx.price_factor: El Paso-metro pricing with Marathon refinery access keeps Socorro gas below the state average

la-porte-tx.refinery_name: Shell Deer Park (4 miles), LyondellBasell Channelview
la-porte-tx.major_employer: Bayport Container Terminal, LyondellBasell, Dow Chemical, Port Houston
la-porte-tx.local_fact: La Porte hosts the Bayport Container Terminal — the largest container facility on the Gulf Coast — and sits at the mouth of the Houston Ship Channel, surrounded by petrochemical plants
la-porte-tx.commute_note: SH-225 (La Porte Freeway) and SH-146 carry dense tanker-truck and refinery worker traffic
la-porte-tx.price_factor: Deep inside the petrochemical corridor — La Porte gas prices are consistently among the cheapest in the Houston metro

del-rio-tx.major_employer: Laughlin AFB, Del Rio ISD, Val Verde Regional Medical Center, CBP
del-rio-tx.local_fact: Del Rio hosts Laughlin Air Force Base — the largest pilot training base in the US Air Force — and sits on the Rio Grande opposite Ciudad Acuña, Mexico
del-rio-tx.commute_note: US-90 and US-277 are the only major highways; local commutes are short but regional freight is minimal
del-rio-tx.price_factor: Remote border town with long distribution distances but cross-border competition; prices track state average

lufkin-tx.university_name: Angelina College, Stephen F. Austin nearby
lufkin-tx.major_employer: Lockheed Martin, Baker Hughes, Angelina County Hospital, Pilgrim's Pride
lufkin-tx.local_fact: Lufkin sits in the Piney Woods of East Texas and hosts Lockheed Martin's Lufkin operations along with historic oilfield-equipment manufacturing legacy
lufkin-tx.commute_note: US-59 (future I-69) is the main Houston-to-Texarkana corridor — heavy truck traffic passes through Lufkin
lufkin-tx.price_factor: East Texas standard pricing with forest-industry truck demand; prices track state average

nacogdoches-tx.university_name: Stephen F. Austin State University (11,000 students)
nacogdoches-tx.major_employer: Stephen F. Austin State University, Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital, Pilgrim's Pride, Elliot Electric Supply
nacogdoches-tx.local_fact: Nacogdoches is the oldest town in Texas — settled in 1779 — and home to Stephen F. Austin State University's 11,000 students
nacogdoches-tx.commute_note: US-59 is the main north-south corridor; local commutes are short and student-dominated
nacogdoches-tx.price_factor: East Texas student-market pricing with moderate chain competition; prices near state average

cleburne-tx.major_employer: Walmart distribution center, Cleburne Medical Center, Fort Worth commuter workforce, Johnson County ranching
cleburne-tx.local_fact: Cleburne is the Johnson County seat on the southwest edge of the DFW metro and hosts a large Walmart regional distribution center
cleburne-tx.commute_note: US-67 northeast to Fort Worth is the primary commute with moderate morning traffic
cleburne-tx.price_factor: Far-south DFW suburb with Walmart and Sam's Club competition keeps prices near or below metro average

seguin-tx.major_employer: CMC Steel (largest steel mill in TX), Caterpillar, Tyson Foods, Guadalupe Regional
seguin-tx.local_fact: Seguin hosts CMC Steel — Texas's largest steel mill — plus Caterpillar and Tyson Foods, making it a key manufacturing hub east of San Antonio on the I-10 corridor
seguin-tx.commute_note: I-10 is the main Houston-to-San Antonio freight corridor and carries heavy daily truck traffic through Seguin
seguin-tx.price_factor: I-10 truck-stop competition keeps Seguin gas prices near the state average

southlake-tx.major_employer: Sabre Corporation HQ, Chesapeake Energy (historic), Central Market, Town Square retail
southlake-tx.local_fact: Southlake has one of the highest median household incomes in the US among mid-sized cities — Southlake Town Square is a model New Urbanist town center and Sabre Corporation's HQ
southlake-tx.commute_note: SH-114 feeds DFW Airport and Las Colinas; Southlake residents have short airport-adjacent commutes
southlake-tx.price_factor: Premium-suburb chain pricing 5-8¢ above metro average; Costco Grapevine is the nearest discount anchor

big-spring-tx.major_employer: Delek US Big Spring Refinery, Federal Correctional Institution Big Spring, Permian Basin oilfield services
big-spring-tx.local_fact: Big Spring hosts the Delek US Big Spring Refinery — 73,000 bbl/day — one of only a handful of inland Texas refineries and the nearest refinery to the Permian Basin crude production
big-spring-tx.commute_note: I-20 carries heavy Permian Basin oilfield freight; Big Spring is a common fuel stop for long-haul West Texas truckers
big-spring-tx.price_factor: Local refinery plus Permian Basin oilfield competition keeps Big Spring gas prices below state average

corsicana-tx.major_employer: Collin Street Bakery (fruitcake maker since 1896), Guardian Industries, Kohler, Russell Stover Candies
corsicana-tx.local_fact: Corsicana is the site of the first commercial oil discovery in Texas in 1894 (27 years before Spindletop) and today is globally famous for Collin Street Bakery's DeLuxe Fruitcake, shipped worldwide since 1896
corsicana-tx.commute_note: I-45 is the main Dallas-to-Houston corridor; Corsicana is a common refueling stop for long-haul traffic
corsicana-tx.price_factor: I-45 midpoint pricing with truck-stop competition keeps Corsicana near or slightly below state average
